review_title: Great product for non developers
description: Great value for money and super exiting to start building once you start to understand the concept a little bit more
author: Luis E.
pros: Ease of use compared to other alternatives, flexibility and ease to integrate with different backends on the same page
cons: It’s still hard to learn but amongst other alternatives and considering its capabilities, it’s perfect

review_title: Design production grade frontends with confidence and speed
description: I built a niche SaaS on WeWeb and moved from idea to a first demo for prospects in very little time. From there I took the MVP to a fully functional and complex platform with minimal changes and without a heavy redesign. The system is aimed at online businesses and online coaches, and it supports running an online summit, managing speakers, scheduling sessions and interviews, and planning the event from start to finish. It lets me organize complex offerings into clear packages and serve dynamic elements automatically so users see what matters at the right moment. WeWeb also helps beyond simple creation by allowing conditional display of elements, which works like a feature flag, so I can expose functionality only to selected users and keep building without disturbing the production environment, or turn on a capability for a small inner circle of testers first.
author: Martin K.
pros: The recent AI features make it remarkably easy to move from a blank canvas to a polished first implementation even if you are not a seasoned frontend developer. The visual builder feels intuitive without dumbing anything down, so you can create interfaces that look professional and feel robust. After a short learning phase the workflow becomes fast and forgiving and designing screens starts to feel natural rather than tedious. Connecting to backend services like Xano, Supabase, or your own REST APIs is straightforward and thoughtfully laid out, which keeps momentum high when you are stitching together data and UI. Beginners find their footing quickly while more advanced builders still get the control they need to structure, scale, and refine. A basic grasp of web technologies, APIs, and REST calls helps smooth the path, but it is not a hard prerequisite to start shipping real products.
cons: At times components behave a little differently across browsers, which is understandable given differences in browser engines, yet it can reduce the sense that everything will just work everywhere. The online documentation is generally strong but can lag behind new feature releases, and in day to day work the community and video resources on YouTube usually fill the gaps quickly. Some official examples lean toward beginners and can leave advanced users wanting deeper patterns and recipes, although with experience and community input you can usually assemble solid solutions. These are small complaints about an otherwise impressive toolset, but they are worth noting if you plan to push the platform hard.

review_title: bad service
description: negative
author: giovanni I.
pros: I don't like their behavior towards the customer
cons: after 7 emails sent in 10 days they never replied or corrected the errors that are on my ecommerce site

review_title: Weweb - The way low-code should be
description: very good when it comes to the platform, their integration capabilities as well as the support system!
author: Marco V.
pros: Ease of use and setup for new users have made it simple for us to adopt. Their assistance from a support side was also very good.
cons: in the beginning there was a steep learning curve but it was expected seeing that there where so many features and functions

review_title: The real deal to build web application.
description: I am satisfied and it has been fun for me, I love learning how to build as well as how things work ⎯ the development and other supporting infrastructure.
author: Rivan S.
pros: It's intuitive! All the function and features work to how I need them to be, or otherwise you can just build your own custom element, function, or component. Also, the team and community have been nurturing each other.
cons: You may need to dig deeper to figure out how things work as their documentation and tutorials may have not catch up to their updates, or what are the alternative of solving your problem.

review_title: Scalable Visual Development Platform
description: Great, we really enjoy the product and have built several applications using it. It's highly performant and affordable for the value.
author: Erik G.
pros: - fully extensible - ability to self host - solid team and support whenever you need it
cons: We don't have any issues with WeWeb. I'd say we're looking forward to more integrations, especially with charts and graphs.

review_title: Definitely building better websites with WeWeb
description: Things that seemed to be difficult while using other CMSs turned out to be easier with WeWeb. My websites performances increased dramatically and it was easy to set up.
author: Andriana Z.
pros: - very visual & the interface is easy to use - no limitation for building your website, allows you to explore & implement your creativity - no need to install or maintain any extension/plugins, everything comes out of the box - the built-in version management system is just amazing - excellent customer experience & support, always available & happy to help
cons: I am one of the early users of WeWeb and back in the days they didn't have enough templates to start with, but now there are plenty of them and the library of pre-built elements keeps growing.

review_title: A powerful visual frontend for real products — not just prototypes
description: WeWeb has been central to building our product. It let us go from concept to MVP in a matter of weeks, with full control over branding, layout, and user logic. We paired it with Xano and Outseta to handle backend and auth, and the stack has worked extremely well. For anyone building a modern SaaS or data-driven frontend, WeWeb is a strong choice — especially if you need flexibility without giving up speed.
author: Jon A.
pros: The best thing about WeWeb is the combination of visual flexibility and backend power. It’s not just a drag-and-drop tool — it allows serious developers to build structured, responsive layouts, integrate with real APIs, and add custom JS where needed. WeWeb’s expression system and logic blocks strike a great balance between no-code and low-code. It let us build a highly dynamic investment platform without needing to compromise on performance or customisation.
cons: Lack of a great integration with a headless CMS makes that part of the product a pain, but once that's built out, it's straightforward.

review_title: Great for Beginners, Room to Grow
description: My overall experience with WeWeb was positive. It’s a great tool for beginners and small projects, though it lacks some advanced features I’d hope for.
author: Ansh R.
pros: The intuitive interface and drag-and-drop functionality made website creation fast and enjoyable, saving time and effort.
cons: Limited customization options for advanced users were frustrating and restricted creative control.

review_title: Super Fast, Powerful, and One of the Best Front-End Tools
description: Our overall experience with WeWeb has been amazing. The platform is powerful, intuitive, and truly accelerates our development process. Their team is also awesome. Super supportive and responsive.
author: Diogo G.
pros: What we like most about WeWeb is how incredibly fast it is to build production-grade applications. You can deploy on your own server, which gives us great flexibility. Honestly, it’s one of the best front-end tools we’ve ever used.
cons: As a partner, one downside is that we need to pay for licenses for our developers. It would be great to have more flexibility around that.

review_title: Marc Gillardin - AOCDTF
author: Marc G.
pros: L'application Weweb est très pratique grâce à son drag & drop. Cette technologie nous à permis de créer un site personnalisé facile et rapide de création.
cons: Nous avons migré récemment sur la V2. Certains modules de la V1 n'ont pas été gardés dans la V2 ce qui est perturbant et parfois problématique.

review_title: WeWeb the Wordpress killer !
author: guilain D.
pros: - Very easy to use ether for non-tech people - Possibility to import our design system in order to have well designed website for all users - high performance of websites - Tech friendly solutions (API, connexion with data bases...) - Product team of WeWeb working with their clients to continuously improve the product
cons: The initial interface, but they are working on a new version !

review_title: The future of front-end development
description: My experience has been largely positive. WeWeb has allowed me to create beautiful, functional applications faster than traditional development methods, making it a valuable tool in my toolkit
author: Sandro S.
pros: The drag-and-drop builder is incredibly powerful and easy to use. I especially appreciate how customizable the platform is, allowing me to create exactly what I need for my clients without compromise.
cons: Occasionally, I encounter bugs when dealing with more intricate animations or design elements, which interrupts the workflow and can be frustrating.

review_title: The best visual coding frontend builder for production applications
description: My experience with WeWeb has been overwhelmingly positive. It’s made the process of building and deploying web apps incredibly fast and easy. I love how much control I have over the design and functionality of my apps. The learning curve was smooth and after a year of use I feel proficient in it thanks to that ease of use.
author: Sergio S.
pros: The drag-and-drop interface is intuitive, and the level of customization WeWeb offers is top-notch. The team listens to the users and the improvements are constant. The new deployment process is one of the greatest things.
cons: Sometimes the integration of custom code can get tricky, especially when managing projects with more complex functionality. A more streamlined workflow for the integration of custom code would be a very welcome improvement.

review_title: The best app front-end builder
description: Great community and customer support team to help us solve a lot of the issues/questions we encounter while developing. We are able to deploy faster and faster new functionalities in our app.
author: Chloe G.
pros: Ability to build very simply the front-end for a complex web application
cons: Most of the cons have been resolved overtime by Weweb, I do not have any cons in mind at this point.

review_title: Very postive long-term use
description: They seem very professional and support is available as needed. My devs have all been able to work off it seemlessly
author: Tamir H.
pros: Intuitive UI and usability; consistent functionality; works great with other tools. Haven't had any issues.
cons: Pricing changed a while ago and I ended up overpaying but unfortunately I wasn't credited. Otherwise great.

review_title: Awesome bubble-rival from Europe
description: Overall, I’ve had a positive experience with WeWeb. The platform has significantly reduced the time it takes to get a project from concept to production, which is a huge benefit for my team.
author: Thomas L.
pros: I love how easy WeWeb makes it to build complex web applications without needing to write a single line of code. The integration with external APIs and databases is seamless, which is crucial for the kinds of projects I work on.
cons: The preview mode can sometimes be a bit unreliable. There have been moments where the live app behaves slightly differently than what’s shown in the editor, which can be confusing.

review_title: The BEST Low-Code Tool for Front End Development
description: Overall, my experience with WeWeb has been great and the team has always gone above and beyond to help us build just about anything we can dream of for our applications.
author: Mike C.
pros: The integration with AI makes writing Javascript and formulas very easy.
cons: The UI could use some improvements and the publishing time is a bit slow, but overall, there's not much to dislike!

review_title: WeWeb - great little underdog website builder with modern features ahead of the rest
description: I was building my new website on Webflow for my coffee roasting business in a desire to move away from Shopify, but didn't like the fact that I couldn't tie my data in directly with airtable, which I already used for inventory management. I stumbled upon Weweb and it solved my problem flawlessly - I could use airtable as a data source for my website, so I wouldn't need to to use workarounds or manually reenter all the product data, it tied in with the fantastic Snipcart so I could have a great checkout experience, and it was easy to use.
author: Alex M.
pros: Weweb is a website builder very similar to other tools such as Webflow or Editor X, but instead of trying to do it all and not being ideal, it focuses on being the best front-end builder it could be. Weweb ties beautifully in to Airtable (and other data sources), meaning I can was able to link it in with the management systems I had already built for my business in Airtable. I was able to build a ecommerce website easily and quickly, with Airtable taking care of all my back-end admin - I can update pages via airtable, I can manage my products via airtable, I can manage my SEO via airtable. It then plugs in neatly with Snipcart for the checkout, which was likewise incredibly simple to do. The developers are very responsive and are happy to help solve any problems I have, and are quick to implement suggestions and feedback.
cons: It's still early days, so there's the odd bug and quirk, but overall nothing really worth mentioning.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Brutus WeWeb a outrepassé César WordPress
description: J'ai construit cinq sites sur WeWeb. Cinq sites en trois mois. A cette déclaration, un de mes professeurs de l'école d'alternance est resté baba. Fort heureusement, on m'a demandé des sites vitrines : j'ai donc pu me concentrer sur la forme plus que sur le fond. L'idée était de déployer assez rapidement des sites qui rentreraient dans un canal de vente. J'ai laissé ma créativité s'exprimer : logos, ton, couleurs... Mes questionnements encore à ce jour concernent le SEO : est-ce que mes efforts (balises, mots clés) seront à un moment payants ? Le vrai plus a été le support technique. Un développeur m'a accompagné tout du long, notamment pour répondre à mes questions. Une formatrice s'est montrée disponible pour une visioconférence de deux heures et demie et a répondu à mes besoins. Je suis content d'avoir pu tester le produit, innovant sur le marché des CMS.
author: Arthur A.
pros: Le menu avant de passer à l'éditeur est ergonomique, il fait très vite oublier WordPress. Le système de checkpoints est appréciable. Une fois les changements sauvegardés, on publie rapidement. Il est facile d'ajouter un collaborateur. Quant à l'éditeur, il fait le café : on construit son site en direct, les pages sont des blocs qui se superposent, les métadonnées sont personnalisables à un endroit. Il est important de prendre en compte la construction de son site en terme de responsive design, ce qui est pris en compte dans l'éditeur. Dans le cas d'ajout de tag, on a accès aux header et body. En définitive, on se dote d'un site vitrine professionnel en un temps record.
cons: J'ai expérimenté des bugs d'affichage, la plupart ont été corrigés au rythme des mises à jour produit. La version mobile des sites est plus compliquée à mettre en page, la formation au logiciel que j'avais reçue n'avait pas balayé outre mesure ce point. A chaque nouveau lancement de l'éditeur, la version anglaise des sites s'affichait constamment, au détriment de la version française. Le format des images m'avait souvent posé problème : trop grandes ou trop petites. J'ai pu avoir le sentiment que les capacités de personnalisation pour les éléments restaient limitées. De manière générale, il est facile de bâtir un site à partir des bloc elements mais le webdesigner est démuni pour des besoins plus poussés.
author_profession: Chef de projet digital en alternance

review_title: A low code leader built to efficiently build web app
author: Gabriel B.
pros: WeWeb's approach allows developers to add logic to pretty much every component of a web app, whether it be with low code formulas or javascript. Workflows, formulas and variables can be managed in a composable way. UI Kits and classes allow for reusing your work, and custom components open the door to developers, would you need to go down that route.
cons: The development process isn't always easy in a team environment, lacking branch only deployments and restoring. Deployed apps sometimes have hard time staying stable/up, but one can always download the code and self host.

review_title: Best Webapp Frontend Development tool I know
author: Antoine G.
pros: The empowerment WeWeb provide is huge. On top of building UI, UX and plug to database very fast, the workflow interface added with the ability to write custom JS everywhere is so powerfull. It's the best low-code frontend option when you consider that the true power of lowcode lies in the ability to develop faster by switching from no code components and logics, to coded components and logics. I started coding using WeWeb <3
cons: Like all "No-Code" they sell that everyone can be a dev now, and launch webapps easily and fast. That's just not true, and when everything you build relies on variables (in WeWeb) I find this speech a bit dishonest. But thats just their marketing right, and every editor does it in the end🤷

review_title: I've build a full Web-To-Store WeWeb Site with no technical skills
description: - Very good contact with team - I would have appreciated to have better information regarding WeWeb backlog - With "Early Bird" pricing, it was a very good deal for me
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: - Drag & drop and absolute "no code" solution really intuitive to manage, I've just added/changed ready-made assets to display my contents (I have NO technical background) - Dev-friendly features gave me high possibilities to connect BDD and APIs to build the full operational Web-To-Store funnel I needed (with help of the WeWeb team) - High-Performances solution
cons: - Lack of tutorial when I used it (it was the very beginning for WeWeb) - The resulting website is full responsive but I had issues with images sizing (trade-offs to do)

review_title: Best user friendly software !
description: - Very user-friendly, so it's perfect for people who don't have any skills or experience in building websites. - Team very helpful and reactive. - Precise analytics of the visits on your website. - Lots of different options for the webdesign (effects, emplacements etc).
author: Victoire F.
pros: I chose WeWeb to build my e-shop, which was complicated for me since I do not have any skills in web design. I was very quickly able to make everything by myself, the software being very user-friendly. I really appreciate that the team was everytime very reactive when I had issues, or questions or just when I needed help. Also, it's possible to link Shopify with WeWeb which was perfect for me since I am using Shopify for the backend. Finally, you have analytics on WeWeb which are very precise since Google Analytics is integrated in the software. I am very satisfied by WeWeb, the software, the team, and the result of my website.
cons: I was dependant of the team in order to link Shopify with my website, since I was not able to make it myself, so it was complicated. There are some actions which are not very instinctive, and you need to ask help to the team - which once again makes you dependant, so takes more time.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Superpower
author: Patrice C.
pros: Weweb allows me to create fully customizable frontend websites to display data from any API (Airtable, Xano…), in complete autonomy. Like a new superpower for me, who's not a dev at all. I definitely love this tool.
cons: Nothing in particular. I just regret not having used it before.
